methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $150M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Rondo Energy, a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by John O'Donnell and Eric Trusiewicz, is evaluated against a peer set of 9 comparables across the Climate & Energy sector—including 1 public company and 8 private firms. The private peer universe spans Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), among others.

Two valuation methods are applied. Stage-Progression, anchored to Rondo's $150M+ total raised, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $285M. Peer-Median uses the 6 same-stage, same-sector comparables, which span $120M to $5.0B with a median of $355M. The wide peer range reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and capital intensity within frontier climate tech.

The triangulated valuation range is $242M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $320M. This reflects reasonable convergence between the two methods, though material uncertainty persists. Key sensitivities include peer selection—the $5.0B Commonwealth Fusion outlier significantly widens the comp range—and the timing and capital intensity of commercial deployment for Rondo's specific technology. Refinement would benefit from clarity on Rondo's technology differentiation and path to revenue relative to named peers.
